definition: Complete trust and confidence in God and His promises. Described as 'the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.' A central theme throughout Scripture.
God's unmerited favor toward humanity. A foundational concept in Christian theology — salvation comes not through human effort but through God's gracious gift through Jesus Christ.

Deliverance from sin and its consequences. In the Old Testament, God saved Israel from physical enemies. In the New Testament, salvation refers primarily to spiritual redemption through faith in Christ.
The greatest virtue and a defining attribute of God. God's love (agape) is unconditional and sacrificial. Jesus summarized the law as loving God and loving one's neighbor.
A binding agreement between God and His people. Key covenants include those with Noah, Abraham, Moses, David, and the New Covenant through Jesus Christ.
The act of buying back or setting free. In Scripture, it refers to God delivering His people from bondage — both physical (Exodus) and spiritual (through Christ's sacrifice).
Communication with God. Includes praise, thanksgiving, confession, and petition. Jesus taught his disciples to pray through the Lord's Prayer and modeled a life of prayer.
Messages from God delivered through prophets. Includes both foretelling future events and forth-telling God's truth. Messianic prophecies pointed to Jesus Christ.
The raising of the dead to life. Christ's resurrection is the foundation of Christian faith and the promise that believers will also be raised to eternal life.
Transgression against God's law and falling short of His standards. It entered the world through Adam and Eve's disobedience, affecting all humanity.
God's sovereign rule over all creation. Jesus proclaimed its arrival and described it in many parables. It is both a present spiritual reality and a future consummation.
The third person of the Trinity. The Spirit inspired Scripture, empowers believers, convicts of sin, and produces fruit (love, joy, peace, etc.) in the lives of Christians.
The act of pardoning offenses. God forgives sins through Christ's sacrifice. Jesus taught that receiving God's forgiveness obligates believers to forgive others.
The ability to apply knowledge rightly. In Scripture, true wisdom begins with the fear of the Lord. Proverbs and Ecclesiastes are dedicated wisdom literature.
Confident expectation based on God's promises. Biblical hope is not wishful thinking but a sure anchor for the soul, especially regarding eternal life and Christ's return.
